questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution provides for the establishment of the Supreme Court?
questions.explanation: Article 32 of the Indian Constitution provides for the establishment of the Supreme Court.

questions.question: The Panchayat Samitis are a part of the three-tier Panchayat system in which state?
questions.explanation: The Panchayat Samitis are a part of the three-tier Panchayat system in West Bengal.

questions.question: Which of the following is the fundamental duty of a citizen to promote harmony and the spirit of common brotherhood amongst all the people of India?
questions.explanation: Article 51(A)(b) of the Indian Constitution is the fundamental duty of a citizen to promote harmony and the spirit of common brotherhood amongst all the people of India.

questions.question: Which of the following is the composition of the West Bengal State Election Commission?
options: The Chief Election Commissioner and 2 Election Commissioners
The Chief Election Commissioner and 3 Election Commissioners
The Chief Election Commissioner and 4 Election Commissioners
The Chief Election Commissioner and 1 Election Commissioner
questions.explanation: The composition of the West Bengal State Election Commission is the Chief Election Commissioner and 3 Election Commissioners.

questions.question: What is the number of Lok Sabha seats allocated to West Bengal?
questions.explanation: West Bengal has 18 Lok Sabha seats.

questions.question: Who led the famous Bengal Volunteers in the Indian independence movement?
questions.explanation: Jatindra Nath Mukherjee was the leader of the Bengal Volunteers.

questions.question: Which of the following Acts was passed by the British government to increase revenue collection from Bengal?
questions.explanation: The Permanent Settlement Act was passed in 1793 to increase revenue collection from Bengal.

questions.question: Who was the President of the Indian National Congress when it passed the Swaraj resolution in 1929?
questions.explanation: Motilal Nehru was the President of the Indian National Congress when it passed the Swaraj resolution in 1929.

questions.question: Which of the following events marked the beginning of the Non-Cooperation Movement in Bengal?
questions.explanation: The Khilafat Movement marked the beginning of the Non-Cooperation Movement in Bengal.

questions.question: Who was the famous Bengali revolutionary who assassinated Sir Curzon Wyllie in 1900?
questions.explanation: Khudiram Bose assassinated Sir Curzon Wyllie in 1900.

questions.question: Which West Bengal Government initiative aims to provide employment to 10 lakh youth through skill development?
questions.explanation: Launched to provide employment to youth through skill development.

questions.question: Which of the following rivers in West Bengal has been declared as a National Heritage River?
questions.explanation: Declared as a National Heritage River due to its historical and cultural significance.

questions.question: In 2025, which of the following projects was approved by the West Bengal Government to boost the state's economy?
questions.explanation: Approved to boost the state's economy and provide employment opportunities.

questions.question: What is the name of the West Bengal Government's flagship program to promote women's empowerment?
questions.explanation: Launched to promote women's empowerment and provide financial assistance.

questions.question: Which of the following states has signed a MoU with West Bengal to promote industrial development?
questions.explanation: Signed to promote industrial development and create jobs in the state.

questions.question: Which of the following West Bengal government schemes aims to provide financial assistance to women for the education of their children?
questions.explanation: Aims to provide financial assistance to women for the education of their children.

questions.question: What is the main objective of the 'Swasthya Sathi' scheme in West Bengal?
options: To provide free medical treatment to all citizens
To provide health insurance to all citizens
To provide free education to all citizens
To provide employment opportunities to all citizens
questions.explanation: To provide health insurance to all citizens.

questions.question: Which of the following schemes is a part of the 'Duare Sashyalaya' initiative in West Bengal?
questions.explanation: Initiative aims to provide government services at the doorsteps of citizens.

questions.question: The 'Sabooj Sathi' scheme in West Bengal aims to provide bicycles to which category of students?
questions.explanation: Aims to promote education among students from economically backward classes.

questions.question: What is the main objective of the 'Kanyashree Prakalpa' scheme in West Bengal?
options: To provide financial assistance to women for the education of their children
To provide employment opportunities to women
To provide health insurance to women
To provide financial assistance to women for their marriage
questions.explanation: Aims to prevent child marriage and promote education among girls.

questions.question: A train covers a distance of 480 km in 8 hours. How much distance will it cover in 24 hours at the same speed?
questions.explanation: Distance = Speed x Time.

questions.question: A bus travels from Kolkata to Digha at an average speed of 80 km/h. How much time will it take to cover a distance of 240 km?
questions.explanation: Time = Distance / Speed.

questions.question: A car travels from Siliguri to Darjeeling at an average speed of 40 km/h. If it covers 60 km in the first 1.5 hours, what is the remaining distance to be covered in the next 3 hours?
questions.explanation: Distance = Speed x Time.

questions.question: A man walks at a speed of 5 km/h. If he covers 15 km in the first 3 hours, what is his speed in the next 2 hours if he covers 10 km?
questions.explanation: Speed = Distance / Time.

questions.question: A train leaves Howrah station at 9:00 AM and reaches Sealdah station at 10:30 AM. If the distance between the two stations is 65 km, what is the average speed of the train?
questions.explanation: Speed = Distance / Time.

questions.question: Pijush's father is the brother of Kajal's grandmother. How is Pijush related to Kajal?
questions.explanation: Pijush is Kajal's sister.

questions.question: Aparna's son is the son of her husband's mother. Who is Aparna's son's father?
questions.explanation: Aparna's son's father is her grandfather.

questions.question: If 'D' is the father of E, E is the son of F, and F is the father of G, then how is G related to 'D'?
questions.explanation: G is the grandson of 'D'.

questions.question: Bimal's daughter is the wife of Suman's son. Then how is Bimal's daughter's son related to Suman?
questions.explanation: Bimal's daughter's son is the son of Suman.

questions.question: Rahul's mother is the daughter of Sushma's father. How is Rahul related to Sushma?
questions.explanation: Rahul is Sushma's mother.

questions.question: A person starts walking from the foot of a hill towards the top. He walks 240 meters due north, then 140 meters due east, then 120 meters due south and finally 200 meters due west. How far is he now from the foot of the hill?
questions.explanation: The person has walked in the form of a square, so distance from the starting point is the length of the square's diagonal.

questions.question: A car travels 7 km north, then 3 km east, then 5 km south and finally 2 km west. How far is the car now from its starting point?
questions.explanation: Final position is 2 km east and 2 km north of the starting point.

questions.question: A man is standing at a place which is exactly in the middle of two churches, one of which is the famous Kalighat Temple of Kolkata. If the man walks 15 meters east, he is 10 meters closer to the Kalighat Temple, and if he walks 20 meters south, he is 15 meters closer to the other church. In which direction is the other church situated?
questions.explanation: The man's original position was equidistant from the two churches and at an angle, so moving south brings him closer to the other church.

questions.question: A tourist is moving in a park. He goes 5 km towards south, then 8 km towards east and then 6 km towards north. In which direction is he now from the starting point?
questions.explanation: The tourist is now 2 km east of the starting point.

questions.question: A boy is standing at the corner of a rectangular field. He walks 15 meters from the corner towards the longer side of the field, then 20 meters towards the shorter side and finally 10 meters back to the shorter side. How far is he now from the starting corner?
questions.explanation: The boy ends up 10 meters along the longer side from the starting corner.
